title: See How Houthis Put the Red Sea at Risk as an Alternative Oil Route
summary: Saudi Arabia has diverted huge amounts of oil to the Red Sea since the Iran war began, but now the Iranian-backed Houthi militants say that they intend to block that route.

title: Iran’s Neighbors Long for a Deal, Any Deal, to End the War
summary: Business owners in Pakistan are getting impatient for a chance to finally trade goods and oil along their long shared border with Iran.

title: Stocks and the Economy Are Increasingly Relying on the A.I. Boom
summary: Investment in artificial intelligence and related companies is lifting the stock market and spending across the economy.

title: Laredo’s Future Rides on Trump’s Next Trade Move
summary: The economy of Laredo, Texas, has boomed under the president’s global tariffs. Now, his demands may threaten its success.

title: Trump’s 50% Tariffs on Canada: What to Know, and What’s Next
summary: The president said punishing 50 percent tariffs would begin by Aug. 19, a significant escalation of the trade war between Canada and the U.S. that he started over a year ago.

title: Mamdani Calls Netanyahu a ‘War Criminal’ and Calls on U.S. to Arrest Him
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani challenged the United States to execute an international arrest warrant against Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel, which New York itself cannot do.

title: Coveted U.S. Nuclear Deal Is a Coup for Saudi Crown Prince
summary: Saudi Arabia had for years sought the controversial deal to obtain American nuclear technology. It is expected to be sealed on Wednesday, deepening U.S.-Saudi ties for decades to come.

title: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Stokes Israeli Concerns of Mideast Arms Race
summary: An Israeli minister sought to downplay the expected deal, while critics said it could spur other regional countries to pursue a nuclear program, and potentially the creation of a bomb.

title: How the Trump Administration’s Nuclear Deal With Saudi Arabia May Work
summary: The Trump administration is expected to announce an agreement with Saudi Arabia on a civilian nuclear program. Such deals, known as 123 Agreements, are designed to prevent the development of weapons.

title: He’s the Last Great Land Artist You’ve Never Heard Of
summary: Charles Ross spent 50 years building “Star Axis,” a naked-eye observatory in New Mexico. Now his masterwork is ready. How to share it with a changed world?

title: Donald Trump Jr.’s Investment Firm Posts Staggering Returns of 200 Percent
summary: The fledgling firm, run by the president’s oldest son and his partner Omeed Malik, has made investments in A.I. and defense technology that have tripled in value in just months.

title: Women Are Too Controlling, She Wrote. Her Message Still Resonates.
summary: The message in “The Surrendered Wife,” the best-selling book by Laura Doyle, was provocative. Many women credit it with saving their marriages.

title: Trump Administration Pressures Some Immigrants to Self-Deport With Fines Up to $1.8 Million
summary: Tens of thousands of immigrants have received letters to pay up or leave, as the government enlists debt collectors and seizes tax refunds.

title: House Republicans Have Tied the Defense Policy Bill to Trump’s SAVE Act
summary: House Republicans have tied their entire legislative agenda to President Trump’s voting restriction measure, as he has demanded.

title: Rubio and Chinese Diplomat Make Plans for Trump and Xi’s Washington Summit
summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio spent much of a meeting with China’s foreign minister discussing the summit, but did not bring up President Trump’s claims of election interference by China.

title: South Carolina and Nevada Fight to Kick Off Democrats’ 2028 Presidential Calendar
summary: The calendar order could be hugely important to the 2028 presidential primary race. Democratic insiders will try to sketch out a schedule this week, and rival states are sniping at one another.

title: Arizona Elections: 6 Takeaways From the Trump-Dominated Primaries
summary: Republicans nominated a former N.F.L. kicker in a top House battleground, a Trump loyalist for governor and an election denier for the state’s top election post.

title: OpenAI Sued Over ChatGPT’s ‘Dangerous’ Health Advice
summary: The case appears to be the first to argue that a chatbot’s advice harmed someone seeking guidance about a medical condition.

title: With 2 Hires, Mamdani Blends Economic Development With Economic Justice
summary: Anthony E. Shorris, a former deputy mayor and a partner at McKinsey, and Lina Khan, the former chair of the Federal Trade Commission, will lead the city’s Economic Development Corporation.

summary: Since Mr. Kirk was gunned down at Utah Valley University, the school and its students have struggled to find a way forward.

title: A Hiker Confronts His Fear of Heights at Ausable Chasm in Upstate N.Y.
summary: A jittery hiker signed up for a via ferrata tour on the cliffs of an Adirondacks gorge. Here’s what he learned on the ledges, rungs and exposed bridges.

summary: Is celibacy cool now?

title: Trump’s New Insult Won’t Save Republicans
summary: The president’s attempt to drum up a third red scare is certain to fail.

title: Dirty Lettuce
summary: We look into the cyclospora outbreak.

title: Louvre Gallery Reopens After Brazen Jewel Heist
summary: Thieves stole crown jewels from the Apollo Gallery at the Louvre Museum in October, forcing its closure. Tourists finally returned on Wednesday.
